claim_id.description: Unique claim identifier (e.g. CLM-20260622-INVOICE-A3F9B1). Required for traceability. Must be echoed exactly in output.

analysis_result_json.description: JSON string output from AG_Claim_Analysis_Core. Contains the 10-field analysis contract: category (Healthcare/Vehicle/Travel), eligible (boolean), documents_complete (boolean), missing_documents (array), analysis_confidence (number), key_document_facts, recommendation, recommendation_notes, suggested_next_action. Parse as JSON before use.

fraud_risk_json.description: JSON string output from AG_Fraud_Risk. Contains fraudRisk object with: level (low/medium/high), score (integer 0-100), score_confidence (number), signals (pipe-separated string), corroborating_signals (pipe-separated string), reasoning, recommended_action. Parse as JSON before use. Note: signals and corroborating_signals are pipe-separated strings, not arrays.

reviewer_package.description: Consolidated multi-section reviewer package from WF_Prepare_Reviewer_Package. Contains validation results, AI claim analysis, fraud risk assessment, and document excerpts. Use as supplementary context if upstream JSONs are incomplete.

knowledge_bundle.description: Loaded KB content from WF_Load_Agent_Knowledge for AG_Decision_Support. Contains decision guidelines, settlement thresholds, approval authority rules, and partial-approval criteria. Read BEFORE applying priority logic. If empty, apply built-in priority logic.

claim_id.description: Echo of the input claim_id. Must match input exactly. Never modify or reformat.

recommendation.description: AI-generated decision recommendation for the human reviewer. Exactly one of: Approve, Partial Approve, Reject, Request Documents. Determined by first-match priority logic. This is guidance only — the human reviewer makes the final decision via HITL.

recommendation_confidence.description: Confidence score 0.0–1.0 for the recommendation. Derived from the combination of analysis_confidence (from AG_Claim_Analysis_Core) and fraud risk score (from AG_Fraud_Risk). High fraud score suppresses confidence. Never use the field name 'confidence' — this field is always named 'recommendation_confidence'.

recommendation_rationale.description: 3–6 sentence explanation of the recommendation. Must reference: the claim category and eligibility outcome, the fraud risk level and score, the specific priority rule that triggered the recommendation, and any key risk factors. No speculation beyond input evidence.

key_risk_factors.description: Ordered list of the most significant risk factors influencing the recommendation. Include fraud signals, validation warnings, missing documents, and confidence gaps. List in descending order of severity. Empty array [] if no risk factors identified.

suggested_settlement_notes.description: Optional guidance for the human reviewer on settlement considerations. Include: if Approve, note the claimed amount and any conditions; if Partial Approve, suggest what portion may be supportable and why; if Reject, state the disqualifying reason clearly; if Request Documents, list the specific documents needed. Keep to 2–4 sentences.
